desc:
Tall, gilded pillars support slender arches, which rise gracefully to the high
ceiling of this large chamber. A raised dais stands at the northern end of the
hall, whilst to the south, an ornate staircase climbs to some unseen room,
above. A smaller stair, half-hidden in a narrow alcove in the north- western
corner of the hall, descends, almost unnoticed.
name: A Ceremonial Hall

desc:
The metallic skeleton of what was once an ornate chandelier sits squarely in the
middle of the hall, but anything of value has long ago been scavenged from its
remains. It is impossible to tell from what great height it might have fallen,
for the ceiling is lost in the omnipresent darkness far overhead.
name: Mahal's Way

desc:
At intervals along this broad passageway, large, smoothly arched alcoves have
been carved into the southern wall. Most now hold little more than dust and
rubble, but in some stand the half-shattered remains of what must once have been
magnificent sculptures.
name: Mahal's Way

desc:
Alcoves are occasionally cut in the walls of this large road, completely empty
except for little piles of alabaster shards -- remnants of shattered statues.
Deep darkness hides the top portions of the pillars and arches that reach up
toward the ceiling.
name: Mahal's Way

desc:
A shattered sculpture is housed in the alcove before you. The entire top half of
the statue has been destroyed, leaving a rough and jagged surface, covered in
dust and splintered fragments of stone. Chunks of polished marble lie about the
base of the statue, and also litter the floor of the passageway itself.
name: Mahal's Way

desc:
The tunnel stops its progress south and makes a sudden turn eastwards. The road
was probably planned to go further south, but it looks like the dwarves hit
harder rock and changed their minds. They left only a small alcove on the
southern wall.
name: Dwarf Road

desc:
Curious echoes reach you here from the long tunnel which disappears from view to
the east, but otherwise the air is still and noiseless. As you look about you,
it is possible to see that the original tunnel has been widened in more recent
times, and small piles of rubble and debris still litter the floor by the
western wall.
name: Dark Tunnel

desc:
This large unlit tunnel cuts an unwavering southern path from here, but ends
abruptly to the north, as it enters a vast cavern. The heavy stone floor has
been cracked and pitted with use, and is covered with fine gravel, but remains
level and firm beneath your feet.
name: Dark Tunnel

desc:
Standing at the front of a large cave, you can see a few broken mine cars and
heaps of slag, the only remaining signs of the long-abandoned activity which
must once have filled this chamber. For this is a silver mine, forgotten since
Durin's day. All of the smaller debris has been cleared away by the new
occupants of this place, and a rough barricade of large loose stones has been
erected in the middle of the cavern, restricting movement to the east to a
difficult scramble. However, passages to the west and south of here appear to be
easily accessible.
name: Chamber of Silver

desc:
Six massive cots line the walls of this smelly cave. Scattered about them are
rinds and old bones, small chunks of rotting flesh, and huge wooden jugs filled
with a foul liquid. An occasional notched club and broken shield add to the
overall impression of clutter. A gargantuan door of solid iron dominates the
south wall of the chamber, whilst the way west is partially blocked by a rough
barrier of rocks and stones. It may be difficult to scramble back out in this
direction.
name: Guard Cave

desc:
A large, dusty and featureless tunnel stretches here from east to west. The
gravelly floor is dry and level, the walls cool and smooth. To the east, the
tunnel opens out into a large cave.
name: Large Tunnel

desc:
Far above you, you sense rather than see the ceiling of this massive natural
cavern, which is hundreds of feet long and over a hundred feet wide. The far
wall lies well beyond the reach of your light, but you get an idea of the huge
dimensions, as you spy the torches mounted in brackets every few feet around the
room. Though originally used by the dwarves of Durin's day, this hall is
obviously now the home to a clan of cave trolls. The air is pervaded by the
foetid odour of their food and faeces, both of which lie in heaps near the
walls. Both of the long north-south walls of this great hall are punctuated by
huge openings into large tunnels. Far away to the north, you can glimpse in the
darkness a raised platform, shadowed in an eerie red light.
name: The Great Hall of the Trolls

desc:
Compared to the Great Hall, which lies to the west of here, this tunnel might
appear cramped, but the ceiling is nonetheless far above your head. The eerie
red-orange glow of a large fire barely penetrates this passage, but it is just
possible to see that the workmanship here is rather crude, suggesting that the
original fine tunnels contructed by the dwarves have since been enlarged by a
rougher hand.
name: Dark Tunnel

desc:
A simple, but elegant archway opens onto a smaller tunnel to the east of here,
whilst this passage continues to the north and south. The walls are slightly
rough, and the floor is quite worn, as if by the trampling of many heavy feet.
The ceiling above you is the only part of this passage which seems untouched by
the marauding trolls, still retaining the original fine workmanship of the
dwarves who crafted it so many years ago.
name: Dark Tunnel

desc:
Perhaps because of its smaller size, this tunnel seems to be one trodden only
infrequently by the trolls, and the floor here shows little sign of wear. To the
west, a simple but elegant archway opens out into a larger tunnel, and to the
east the passageway curves north, out of view.
name: Dark Tunnel

desc:
With a graceful sweep, the passage here turns from west to north. No sharp
corners mark this transition, but rather an almost imperceptible arc, whose
definition is lost in the blanketing darkness just beyond the reach of your
light.
name: Dark Tunnel

desc:
The fine workmanship of the dwarven craftsmen, who contructed this tunnel so
many years ago, seems unmarked by the passage of time. The path here is bold and
straight, the floor smooth and perfectly level, and above you the ceiling makes
no dip or rise, but maintains a perfect parallel to the ground below.
name: Dark Tunnel

desc:
A gleaming throne, wrought from a single huge piece of obsidian, rests here upon
a raised rock dais. Unearthly shadows dance around you, cast in the hellish red
light of a massive pyre. Brighter than the torches mounted in brackets along the
walls of this hall, the firelight reaches far above you, and yet still the roof
of this cavern is more imagined than seen.
name: The Obsidian Throne

desc:
Here, at the southern end of the Great Hall, the lofty ceiling describes a
massive downward arc, becoming the southern wall. This wall is broken by a
single exit, framed to either side by massive stone pillars, carved more for
effect than for structural support. Huge torches are mounted beside these
pillars, and the light they cast flickers in the breeze from the corridor
beyond. Rows of smaller torches are mounted on the walls throughout the hall,
but their light falls away too rapidly for you to see much of this cavernous
chamber in detail. This end of the hall seems little used, but from further
north an unpleasant stench assails you.
name: The Great Hall of the Trolls

desc:
Many feet have passed this way before yours, and the floor of this spacious
tunnel has been worn smooth by heavy use. A huge stone slab to the west of you
blocks a dark entryway, but to the east and south the tunnel branches into two
similar echoing passageways.
name: Wide, Dark Tunnel

desc:
In a single sweeping arc, the passageway here switches from a north south
direction to a curving eastward path. The floor beneath you is quite smooth and
perfectly level, the air cool and still, and all about you the darkness reigns
supreme.
name: Dark Tunnel

desc:
Smooth and flawless, the cold rock walls, ceiling and floor here bear no scars
from the lengthy occupation of this part of Moria by hoards of barbaric trolls.
The deep darkness has a strange air of tranquility, and even your soft foot-
falls seem booming and graceless. Peering through the blackness, it is just
possible to see that the path you tread stretches away from you to the north,
and snakes around smooth curves to the west of here.
name: Dark Tunnel

desc:
Small, but well-proportioned, this dark tunnel shares the compelling quiet of
the cavern beyond, to the north. But the smooth, flawless walls seem to speak
volumes in their silent, ageless testimony to the immeasureable talent of the
dwarven artisans, whose adroit hands long ago carved this thoroughfare from the
living rock.
name: Dark Tunnel

desc:
A slight putrescent smell diffuses through the air here. Almost lost in this
cavernous tunnel, the stench grows a little stronger toward the south, where a
rough aperture opens out into a large cave beyond. To the north, the tunnel
follows a straight, and well-worn path.
name: Wide, Dark Tunnel

desc:
The air from the wide tunnel to the north seems fresh and wholesome compared to
the stomach-churning reek here. Though your eyes are watering a little, it is
still possible to make out that the ceiling of this cavern rises more than fifty
feet above the ground. A faint bloody trail on the gravelly floor leads through
a wide opening to the east, but the dark cleft in the southern rock- face could
easily escape attention.
name: Very Large Cave

desc:
This smallish cave is clearly some sort of grisly larder. Bones are scattered
across the floor, and the sickly-sweet odor of decaying flesh permeates the air.
Your eyes are drawn to the disgusting array of corpses, hanging half- rotten
from hooks along the north wall of this revolting room. It is no longer possible
to guess the identities of these hapless victims to trollish gluttony, but it is
clear that not all of these carcasses are animal in origin.
name: Food Store

desc:
A faint pungent smell wafts toward you here from the east, but this tunnel is
otherwise unremarkable. Though the walls and high ceiling are fairly rough, the
floor has been worn into a smooth rut from the frequent passage of heavy feet
along a never-changing east west pathway.
name: Wide, Dark Tunnel

desc:
Several things about this spot attract your attention, not least of all the hot
acrid air, which drifts from the chamber to the east. An aura of corruption
seems to assault you as you contemplate the ornate opening in the north wall of
this passageway, and crude silvered letters can be seen, sparkling on the twin
columns of this entrance.
name: Wide, Dark Tunnel

desc:
You are teetering on the very rim of a large pit, where the trolls toss the
remains of their dead after burning them. Warm upcurrents of air from the chasm
carry small particles of ash, which seem to cling all over you, making your nose
run and your eyes sting. But that is not all: a strange sinister presence seems
to rise from the depths of the crater, enshrouding the whole chamber in gloom.
name: Grave Pit

desc:
It is obvious that this room was originally constructed by the dwarves, as the
high walls and curved ceiling are smooth and finished, and the hexagonal floor
is covered by an intricate pattern of black and scarlet stone tiles. However
crude alcoves have been clumsily chiselled into the otherwise featureless walls,
and in each of these can be seen a grotesque stone sculpture, depicting some
hideous act performed by the trolls throughout the ages, in the name of their
dark master. To the north, an elegant archway leads to the sanctuary and altar.
name: Temple of the Dark Lord

desc:
You stand at the altar to the Dark Lord. A miasma of evil fills this place,
making even the stout-hearted feel faint. The altar itself is a huge silver
cauldron on a raised stone platform. It is in this that the trolls boil alive
their sacrifices, sometimes animal, but sometimes an unfortunate prisoner. In an
alcove set into the north wall, the skulls and broken helms of famous western
heroes are proudly displayed as a token to previous victories.
name: Altar to the Dark Lord

desc:
Your footsteps echo back at you in this dead-end tunnel, but otherwise a deathly
hush seems to fill the air. The floor here is less worn than in the main
passageways behind you, but it seems that this is no disused walkway, and to the
east a well-crafted, heavy stonedoor seems to invite exploration.
name: Wide, Dark Tunnel

desc:
Rectangular in shape, and well-crafted, this is the private chamber of Bulag,
High Priest of the Temple to Sauron. The floor and walls here are of highly
polished stone, and at the centre of the room stands an imposing stone table.
Many tokens of high status amongst the trolls are scattered on the table, and
also in heavy wooden chests, which line one wall. At the eastern end of this
cave a small curtained alcove houses Bulag's bed.
name: Bulag the Priest's Cave

desc:
Anguish seems to become incarnate in this room, which is filled by the ragged
husks of slaves, whose spirits long ago left their miserable bodies. The air
reeks of death and decay, the floor is caked with blood, and small insects
scurry hither and thither, infesting all who enter this dark and squalid place.
name: Slave Pit

desc:
You have found the secret treasure chamber of the trolls. No petty hoardings
clutter this room; instead small heaps of coins, gems and valuables are housed
in a number of sturdy wooden chests which line one wall. Here is kept the best
of the plunder, which the pillaging trolls have collected over many long years
of fighting and raiding.
name: The Secret Treasure Hoard

desc:
The Emerald Way widens out here, into a broad chamber, with a high, domed
ceiling. A massive, natural rift cleaves the roof in the northern part of the
chamber, but the wide stone stairway which spirals up toward this fault is
obstructed by a huge block of masonry, which impedes progress to the level
above.
name: Bottom of a Stairwell

desc:
Narrow, but well-wrought, this passage was clearly hewn from the stone of the
mountain, by the dwarves, for their own exclusive use. The tunnel is narrow, but
its walls are smooth, and the floor level. The ceiling, which is quite low,
describes a graceful arch above your head.
name: A Winding Accessway

desc:
The stair climbs high above, and plunges downward into the unseen depths below.
The rumble of fast-flowing water can be heard quite clearly, and a faint mist
rises, to refresh the stale air which fills this passageway. The cold, dark,
stone walls are beaded with moisture, which seeks out every tiny flaw and
crevice, and each tread of the spiralling staircase is covered by a sheen of
water, with tiny pools collecting in any indentation.
name: A Narrow, Winding Stair

desc:
Perhaps, in their destructive frenzy, Sauron's foul followers ran straight past
the statue, which stands in the nearest alcove, for somehow it remains almost
completely intact. This large, and finely-wrought work of art is obviously a
representation of Aule, himself.
name: Mahal's Way

desc:
The distant sound of dripping water echoes from the west, but no other sounds
intrude upon the stillness of this long passageway, which stretches east, into
the darkness. Large alcoves have been cut, every few paces, into the southern
wall, but most of these now stand empty, except for a little dust and debris.
name: Mahal's Way

desc:
A series of massive, arched, stone bridges once supported wide roadways, which
spanned this lofty cavern. However, most of these are now little more than
crumbling ruins, and the broad path, upon which you stand, comes to an abrupt
end, just to the west. The only way to traverse the chasm ahead is by way of a
network of smaller, and apparently less secure, cat-walks.
name: Mahal's Wells

desc:
This narrow and chipped stone cat-walk seems precarious indeed. Even the dwarves
seem to have needed some reassurance as they trod this path, since a series of
wooden posts line the route, and suspended between these, by means of stout
metal hoops, is a thick, rusty chain. Far below, the floor of the chamber is
filled by an uninviting pool, made red-brown by ancient deposits of iron.
name: Mahal's Wells

desc:
The cat-walk terminates abruptly here, in a crumbling and treacherous mass of
stone. Twenty feet away it resumes its course, but between here and there, a
gaping chasm blocks the way. The heavy iron chain, which forms the handrail
here, is rusted and clearly weakened with age, perhaps to the point of being
useless. It would seem that the danger of falling into the stagnant red-brown
pool, below, is very real indeed.
name: Mahal's Wells

desc:
A dreary red-brown pool is cradled in the rock of the cavern floor, far below
this narrow cat-walk. Chips and flakes of stone lie scattered about, in a silent
testimony to the age and fragility of the path, and the old, rusty chain, which
forms a handrail, seems to offer little guarantee of safety.
name: Mahal's Wells

desc:
A narrow, granite stair once clung to the north wall of this cave, but now all
that remains is an uneven pile of broken rock, and a few rough out-croppings of
stone, connected to the bridge overhead. The tattered remnants of an old rope
are still attached to one of these prominences, swinging gently in the cool
breeze, far above.
name: Beneath a Stone Cat-walk

desc:
This room is quite large, and is architecturally different from the adjacent
areas. Above is a lustrous black dome in which can be seen the seven stars of
the Crown of Durin -- silver shining so brightly it seems to illuminate the
passage. Cut into the northern wall is a narrow flight of black steps leading
upward to some sort of chamber. On the southern wall is a detailed carving of a
dwarf wielding an axe, his eyes two coal-black gems.
name: Watcher's Hall

desc:
The wide road traverses the darkness from the west to the east. Somewhere in the
roof water drips down and forms some puddles on the floor, adding to the overall
humidity in here. There are fragments of stone and rocks lying all over the
floor.
name: Wide Road

desc:
The majestic columns and mosaic patterns characteristic of this portion of
Khazad-dum have here been totally destroyed. The main road continues its
east-west course, but to the south a rough-hewn passageway has been brutally
carved into the rock. North is a passage apparently of dwarvish origin, but from
that direction comes a horrific charnel-house stench. A large portion of the
roof has fallen here, and from the aperture left behind a steady flow of water
drops, eventually finding its way to a narrow opening in the floor.
name: At a Nexus

desc:
The wide road leaps in a majestic arch over the chasm of Durin's Chimney. The
large stone bridge is in excellent shape despite the many earthquakes that have
shaken this area over the years. The great central column of the chim- ney -- an
artificial construct engineered by the dwarves -- intersects the bridge at its
midpoint, and it seems that a portion of the long stair that wound about the
column at one time has survived here.
name: Durin's Chimney

desc:
A wide stone bridge crosses this chasm in a majestic arch. In the centre of the
bridge, the narrow central column built by the dwarves is actually incorp-
orated into the design of the bridge - splitting it as a large boulder might
divide a stream. Once the entire length of Durin's Chimney was accessible by
using a spiral stair that encircled the central pillar, but most of it was
probably destroyed years ago. A small section seems to have survived here,
however, and it may be possible to utilise the stair to access the lower levels.
name: Durin's Chimney

desc:
The majestic road to the east terminates here in a broad flight of stairs that
lead down into darkness. North and south runs a smaller thoroughfare, disap-
pearing in both directions into the inky blackness of the depths. The stairs are
of note, because they appear to have been carved from a single block of hard
black obsidian -- craftmanship not seen on Arda since the Second Age.
name: Obsidian Stairs

desc:
The floor here is completely covered by blooms of blood-red algae. The air is
very damp, and droplets of water bead and drop from the ceiling. The roof here
looks very fragile, as the water is seeping through myriads of tiny fractures.
The floor must slope imperceptibly toward the south, for the moisture seems to
be draining in that direction.
name: Dark Road

desc:
You are standing in a dark passage, going north-south. Moss and lichen cling to
the walls, living on water dripping from the roof. The floor is quite slippery
due to the thin film of water, and due to the hard, highly polished material
from which the floor is constructed.
name: Dark Road

desc:
A large basalt block has fallen from the ceiling, revealing a dark hole from
which issues a small but steady rivulet of water. The floor is covered by a
shallow pool of water that seems to be draining toward the southwest through
cracks in the floor. The corridor to the north appears to be damp as well, but
to the east the darkness is almost palpable, blotting out all traces of light.
The floor beneath the murky pool is extremely slick due to some sort of algal
growth, while the walls and rubble from the ceiling have been claimed by exotic
fungi of various colours.
name: Bend in the road

desc:
This hall is extremely dark, its black surfaces seeming to eat all light. The
prevailing gloom makes it hard for one to get one's bearings, but the hall seems
to continue in an east-west fashion.
name: Dark Hall

desc:
The darkness here is almost palpable, swallowing all light and hungering for
more. The lack of visual stimuli makes it extremely difficult to become oriented
-- in every direction is only the omnipresent darkness.
name: Dark Hall

desc:
This extremely dark hall runs east and west. The walls are of deep black stone
that seems to devour light -- though the passage is at most ten metres wide, its
sides are barely discernible in the gloom.
name: Dark Hall

desc:
The stench here is overwhelming, arising from a pile of headless orc corpses
decomposing in the southeastern corner. A rickety table in the centre of the
room contains the remains of a recent meal, that, judging by the amount of blood
on the table and floor, was eaten while the meat was rather fresh. Adding to the
horrible atmosphere are an assortment of wicked knives and hooks probably used
to torture prisoners.
name: Burzruth Outpost

desc:
This is the place where the Uruks sleep, if they sleep at all. Crudely designed
beds are lined up against the eastern wall. Bits of armour, clothes and weaponry
are scattered on the floor. In the middle of the room, the fur of some huge and
unlucky beast lies in the filth.
name: Barracks

desc:
Several decomposing corpses, apparently once orcs, are stacked here. All the
corpses are missing their heads, and there is no sign of them here. The bodies
are riddled with wounds, suggesting the orcs died in combat. On the western wall
a crude trellis has been erected, with several dozen scalps tied to the
crosspieces in a gruesome display of the Burzruth's skill at arms. Clearly
visible behind the trellis is a hole where the masonry has been shattered and
removed.
name: Back Room

desc:
The narrow passage continues north-south here. South of here there appears to be
an intersection, while to the north the passage becomes even narrower. There are
piles of rubble everywhere, and sharp jagged rocks jut out from the rough-hewn
walls.
name: Narrow Passage

desc:
You are creeping along a small passage going north-south. The rocky walls are
sharp and clumsily crafted, suggesting that this passage was cut after the
dwarves left Khazad-dum. Faint noises can be heard from the south, but they are
too soft to identify.
name: Narrow Passage

desc:
The smell from the north is fast becoming unbearable, overpowering the nose and
bringing to mind opened graves. The floor is extremely sticky and is coated with
a brownish substance that could be dried blood. Bizarre eyeless insects crawl
the walls and floor, seemingly feeding on the loathsome residue on the floor.
name: A Smelly Passage

desc:
The large pile of gutted corpses that almosts blocks the passageway here is
probably the source of the foul, cloying odour that dominates this area. The
carcasses are in various states of decay, those on top appearing freshly
butchered, while those on the bottom are little more than bones surrounded by
seething hordes of squirming larvae. The floor and walls seem alive with hosts
of blind beetles, crawling through cracks in the floor from some horrid abyss
below to feed here.
name: A Smelly Passage

desc:
The long stone tables flanked by wooden benches indicate this room was perhaps
used by the dwarves as a dining hall in ages past. Unappetizing piles of smelly
meat lying on the tables suggest that maybe the current inhabitants of Moria use
this place in a similar manner, even though the smell of corruption coming from
the corridor to the south is overwhelming. A breeze originating somewhere to the
east brings with it the distinct odour of fresh blood and burning meat.
name: Mess Hall

desc:
Obviously a kitchen of dwarven origin, this place is still used as a food
preparation area for the orcs inhabiting this portion of Moria. Several recently
butchered corpses of undetermined race are stacked against the south wall and a
pile of various bodily organs lies next to the bodies, housed in a large metal
basin. Some of the ovens are currently in use, for the smell of roasting meat
fills the air. A strong breeze originates in a shaft above the row of ovens,
clearing the room of excess smoke and carrying the aroma of cooking meat down
the hall to the west.
name: Kitchen

desc:
The stairs end here, levelling out into a flat passage to the west. The floor
here is dingy and appears worn by frequent use. The room to the west looks to be
a kitchen, perhaps accounting for the heavy use of this tunnel.
name: Top of Low Stairs

desc:
You are standing on some broad and low stairs. On the right side of the stairs,
there is a shallow incline probably used for dragging wagons up and down. The
passage seem to be well-trodden although it does not contain any special
craftmanship.
name: Low Stairs

desc:
Shallow alcoves cut into both walls of this passageway contain debris that at
one time probably made up a collection of shelves and racks for storage. There
are a few broken pieces of metal strewn about that suggest maybe armour and
weapons were kept here. From the east a breeze is blowing, bringing with it the
fetid smell of decay mixed with the hint of roasting meat. To the east a large
area containing several large tables can be discerned, while westward the broad
tunnel fades into the gloom.
name: Entrance to the Mess Hall

desc:
This broad passage continues its east-west course, its roof lost in the inky
blackness overhead. The floor here is coated with layers of some sort of sticky
substance, and from the cavernous heights above, a faint rustling can be
periodically heard. From the east a soft wind brings with it the stench of
putrefying meat.
name: Broad Road

desc:
The floor of this place is covered in filth. Scattered about are the bones of
innumerable rodents and other small animals and the whole mess is coated in an
extremely sticky substance. It is impossible to make out the ceiling here, and
from the darkness above a faint rustling can sometimes be heard. There is a
light and steady breeze coming from the east bringing a rather strong odour of
something rotting.
name: Broad Road

desc:
This wide road is not as ornamental as other passages in Moria, suggesting it
was used by dwarves for more mundane purposes. Westward, the road appears to
end, while to the east it marches into the night. The ceiling here must be very
high for the walls extend up until they are swallowed by darkness. The slightest
movement here causes echoes to reverberate down the passage, and sometimes a
slight rustling overhead seems to answer. From the east, a slight breeze carries
with it the odour of rotting meat.
name: Broad Road

desc:
The dark alley comes to a corner here, continuing both south and east. One of
the large basalt blocks that make up the floor seems to be missing. In its stead
a small tunnel disappears down into the rock. The unmistakable odour of
decomposing flesh is being borne along by a chilly breeze originating somewhere
to the east.
name: Bend in the road

desc:
This darkened alley continues both north and south. The black basalt of the
walls and floor has been gouged and scored in several places, perhaps by
vandalous orcs delighting in the destruction of beauty. A slight breeze from the
north carries a hint of rotting meat.
name: Dark Alley

desc:
This side-passage is very dark, its surfaces made of giant dark blocks of basalt
that reflect very little light. It opens into a much larger area to the south,
while a faint draft is detectable flowing out of the darkness to the north.
name: Dark Alley

desc:
This narrow passage slopes down at a sharp angle from the floor of the hall
above. Tiny rivulets of water clinging to the steep floor meander downward into
the depths below, bringing nourishment to several large slimy patches of
blood-red algae.
name: Narrow Crawlspace

desc:
The narrow tunnel reaches its nadir here -- proceeding east or west will take
you upward again. Water trickling down the passageway from the east has gathered
here and formed a puddle overlying several inches of cold soupy mud. Beneath the
deposit of mud cracks in the rock can be felt, none wider than a finger, which
must allow the water to drain from this area.
name: Deep Tunnel

desc:
This narrow tube plunges downward at a steep angle from the floor of the dark
alley above. The rough walls indicate that this passage was probably not carved
by dwarves, but you cannot ascertain whether it is natural or created by the
current foul inhabitants of Moria.
name: Narrow Crawlspace

desc:
This natural crack terminates in a hole below -- proceeding in that direction
requires a drop of a little more than a metre onto a pile of rubble. A steady
stream of water rushes out of the darkness from atop the incline leading west,
dropping through the hole below into a pool. The floor and walls here are very
slick with red algae.
name: Narrow Crack

desc:
From here, the narrow passage drops down both east and west. The rocks are very
slick in this area, due to the water steadily dropping from a tiny crack in the
ceiling. The water drains in both directions, gurgling down into darkness.
name: Peak in the Passage

desc:
This narrow crack in the rock extends upward to the west at a fairly steep
angle. Below is as drop of about a metre onto a pile of rubble in what seems to
be a large area. The walls of this space are slick with growths of blood- red
algae, and the footing here is made more treacherous by water seeping through
cracks in the roof and flowing out through the opening below.
name: Narrow Passageway

desc:
Judging by the filthy mats and scattered weaponry lying about, this room is
heavily used by the orcs of this part of Moria. A circular chalk outline in the
centre of the room brings to mind an arena, an impression augmented by chalk
tallies crudely scrawled on the walls, perhaps serving to keep score. The corpse
of a small orc, only a little over one metre in height, has been tossed in the
corner, blood still seeping from a jagged wound in the abdomen.
name: Upper Barracks

desc:
This shaft vanishes into the raven-black darkness overhead, its sides too smooth
to allow one to continue upward. A brisk breeze of fresh air whistles faintly as
it flows past the opening and into the room below, bringing to mind the pristine
snowfields of the mountainside somewhere far above.
name: In an Airshaft

desc:
Shelves are set high about the walls of this treasure chamber. Set atop them,
and scattered carelessly in heaps about the floor is all manner of loot, much of
which is little better than junk, but some of which might command a price:
haphazard piles of brass buttons and old wooden pipes sit among pots full of
coins, and piles of weapons, shields and armour, most of which is too small for
any troll to use.
name: Trolls' Treasure Cave

desc:
This is a strange chamber indeed, with walls lined completely with neatly
stacked piles of bones. Long bones have been layered at right-angles to the
walls, to form stepped platforms. The bases of these benches are formed from
elven, half-elven and human bones, whilst the shorter limbs of dwarves and
halflings comprise the upper tiers. Scattered on top of this are countless
skulls, laughing out at you from between a forest of ribs. In places, the bones
have been arranged in macabre patterns. A few old, dry skins hang on the walls,
above the bones, completing this grotesque celebration of past glorious battles.
name: An Ossuary

desc:
The icy grasp of this polluted pool is numbing. The cold, granite walls are
smooth, and glisten with moisture, offering no hand-holds. The red-brown water
remains too deep for wading, even at the periphery of the cavern, and a slight
metallic smell assails your nostrils.
name: A Stagnant Pool

desc:
The unfathomable depths of this frigid pool defy imagination. The water itself
is red-brown, heavy with deposits of iron, leached from the bedrock over
countless centuries. And, like icy fingers, the water seems to grasp at your
limbs, its chill touch numbing them almost instantaneously.
name: A Stagnant Pool

desc:
The air here feels heavy with moisture, which seems to drift in from the east.
The cold, stone floor, the straight, tall walls, and the smooth ceiling, above,
all glisten with a thin film of water, which intensifies the slightest sparkle,
dancing from the minute flecks of bright green beryl, embedded in the dull, grey
granite.
name: The Emerald Way

desc:
Cold, wet walls rear up on all sides of this mighty cavern, which is spanned by
a network of narrow, and apparently, rather fragile catwalks. Though the path
here is quite broad and firm, the only way to traverse this chamber is via one
of those slender footbridges, which seem to be suspended miraculously above the
dank, red-brown pools, known as Mahal's Wells.
name: Mahal's Wells

desc:
An exquisite black and gold staircase spirals down from above, terminating in
the cool black basalt of the floor. This chamber is fairly large, with a huge
domed ceiling rising above to merge with the tube from whence the staircase
descends. A pair of huge stone doors is set in the wall to the east, both doors
propped open with grisly pyramids of heads torn from the necks of unlucky orcs.
Passages to the north and west have been almost sealed off with crude ramparts
built of loose stone.
name: At the Base of a Staircase

desc:
This majestic road stretches off into the darkness eastward. Huge pillars line
the walls, reaching up to dizzying heights before being lost in the darkness
above. Along the walls between the pillars are carvings of dwarven heroes, shown
in battle wielding huge axes and war-hammers. The floor is tiled in a mosaic of
elaborate geometric patterns, still undamaged despite the passage of unnumbered
years.
name: Wide Road

desc:
Puddles and rubble cover the floor of the corridor here. To the east it looks as
if a portion of the ceiling has fallen for the debris is much more prominent in
that direction. A path has been kept clear here, apparently this corridor is
still used. The statues lining the wall are completely encased in skins of mould
and lichen, hastening the demise of these works of beauty.
name: Wide Road

desc:
The road ends to the east in a large rampart of rocks, loosely piled to block
further progress to the east. The roof is much lower here than in the passage to
the west -- the ominous black stone hovers just overhead, pressing down toward
the floor.
name: Dark Road

desc:
The grand roadway passes west-east here. Huge pillars are lined along the walls
and busts of famous dwarves are placed on cutouts in the walls. Between some
pillars there are also complete statues of dwarves, depicted in their full
armoury and glory.
name: Wide Road
